# Spanfold

> Temporal interval comparison for application state. Records when a predicate held for a key, then compares those periods across sources, providers, and pipeline stages.

Spanfold is a library for recording state windows — half-open intervals where a defined condition held — and running structured temporal comparisons across multiple sources. A predicate can be any condition: a boolean flag, a threshold crossing, a status code change, or any value transition. Spanfold tracks the period it held and closes the window when it no longer does.

It is designed for problems where the question is not "what is the current value?" but "when did each source believe this condition was true, and where did they differ?"

## Core problems it solves

- **Multi-provider outage comparison**: which monitoring provider reported a degraded period that another missed, reported late, or recovered from early?
- **Pipeline stage divergence**: where did a condition appear at one processing stage but not another, arrive late, or drop before the final stage?
- **No-future-leakage backtesting**: auditing past decisions using only data that was observable at the time, without accidentally including observations that arrived later.

## Comparators

Overlap, Residual (target-only), Missing (comparison-only), Gap, Coverage, Symmetric difference, Containment, Lead/Lag, As-of.

- [Overview](https://scottlr.github.io/Spanfold/index.html)
- [Get started](https://scottlr.github.io/Spanfold/get-started.html)
- [Use cases](https://scottlr.github.io/Spanfold/use-cases.html)

- [Visual Auditing](https://scottlr.github.io/Spanfold/visualiser.html)

- [Comparison guide](https://github.com/Scottlr/Spanfold/blob/main/docs/comparison-guide.md)

## Packages

- [.NET / C# — reference implementation](https://github.com/Scottlr/Spanfold/blob/main/packages/dotnet/README.md): complete API surface, CLI, testing helpers, samples, benchmarks
- [Python — partial port](https://github.com/Scottlr/Spanfold/blob/main/packages/python/README.md): early typed port following the same behavioral model
